industryFaqs.question: Why is practical computer literacy lacking in school students?
industryFaqs.answer: While school students are highly adept at swiping smartphones, most lack keyboarding touch skills, computer shortcut knowledge, file organization abilities, and cloud collaboration techniques. School computer labs often prioritize rote textbook theory due to class time constraints.

industryFaqs.question: What does NEP 2020 say about computer education?
industryFaqs.answer: India's National Education Policy 2020 mandates the integration of coding, mathematical logic, and computational thinking starting from Class 6, preparing students for an AI-driven economic landscape.

industryFaqs.question: Is it safe for children to use generative AI tools?
industryFaqs.answer: Yes, if guided within a secure educational framework. SkillNest teaches students safe search limits, strict privacy rules (never sharing personal data with bots), and structured prompt templates.

industryFaqs.question: Why is EdTech shifting from video lessons to live cohorts?
industryFaqs.answer: Recorded video classes suffer from low completion rates (under 10%) due to a lack of accountability. Interactive live cohorts with small groups (5-10 kids) keep students engaged and allow real-time debugging.

industryFaqs.question: How do online cohorts help bridge regional education gaps?
industryFaqs.answer: Online platforms allow students in smaller cities (like Durg, Bhilai, or regional towns in J&K/Sikkim) to access the same quality of premium instruction and modern tech curricula as students in metro areas.
